messages.body:
Dear Mr Al-Habsi,

Just sharing Layla's April highlights and one area to support.

Layla had a standout month in Chemistry and Mathematics — top of class on the equilibrium unit test and consistently strong on calculus. Her oral-communication rubric climbed from 3.4 to 4.0, the third month running, supported by her Model UN preparation.

The one to support: written Arabic. Her score dipped below 3.0 for the second month. Ms Khadija prepared a 3-week scaffold pack and Layla has 2 of 12 sessions done so far. Quiet encouragement at home goes a long way here.

Happy to schedule a chat any time.

Best,
Sandra

Dear Ms Swart,

Thank you for the lovely note. Layla shared the scaffold pack with me — could we schedule a short chat next week to talk through her written-Arabic plan? Mornings work best.

Best,
Mahmoud Al-Habsi
Of course — how about Wed 22 May at 09:30? A 15-min slot in my free period. I'll send a calendar invite if that works.

The pack is in Layla's locker. She'll start with prompt 1 this Friday. 15 minutes per session is plenty — no need to push longer. Friday check-ins through May.

If you want to see what she's writing, ask her to share the first scaffold this Sunday.

— Khadija

Layla's Chemistry mid-term sits on 12 May at P3 (Lab 1). Format: 50-question paper, 90 minutes.

The revision pack is available from Mr Salim — Layla picked one up last week.

Don't forget to confirm any accommodations (extra time, reader) by Friday 8 May.

subject: Re: Omar's attendance — could we chat?

messages.body:
Hi Mr Al-Habsi,

Omar has missed 3 days this month without a note. Mr Saab raised a separate flag about disengagement in class on Tuesday.

Could we arrange a short call this week so we're on the same page about how to support him? Manhaj's tracker pattern shows the absences cluster around Mondays + post-exam days — happy to talk through what we're seeing.

Best,
Sandra
Term 3 football is open for sign-up. Practice runs Tue + Thu 16:00-17:30. Match-day Saturdays optional. Form due Friday 9 May.
Omar signed up. Match-day Saturdays count us in too. Thanks!
Omar's Maths quiz lands on 14 May at P3. Topics: percentages, ratios, simple linear equations. 45-minute paper.

Yasmin had a wonderful time on stage today — sharing a few photos from the morning. She was particularly proud of remembering the second verse.

Thank you for the costume help.

Summer day-camp opens 15 July. Three weeks, OMR 40 optional add-on (included as a line item in the Term 3 invoice if you'd like to enrol Yasmin).

Reply by 15 June to confirm.

subject: Term 2 invoices · OMR 1,820 outstanding household total

messages.body:
Reminder that the remainder of your Term 2 invoices is due 25 May.

Layla: OMR 750 outstanding.
Omar: OMR 1,070 outstanding.
Yasmin: paid in full.

Household total: OMR 1,820.

Pay via the Manhaj Invoices tab, bank transfer, or in person at the front desk. Receipts auto-emailed once processed.

Parent-teacher evening on Wednesday 18 May from 16:00. You can book slots from your dashboard.

Ms Swart (Layla's advisor) has 3 slots remaining.
Mr Yousef (Omar's PE coach) has 5 slots remaining.
Ms Aida (Yasmin's class teacher) has 6 slots remaining.

School closes for the Eid Al-Adha holiday from 24 June (Thursday) through 26 June (Saturday). Classes resume Sunday 27 June.

End-of-year ceremony 30 June, 18:00, school auditorium. All families welcome.

subject: May monthly reports · sent to all parents

messages.body:
April's monthly reports are now in your Dashboard for each of your children.

Layla: strong month overall · one area to support (written Arabic).
Omar: improving in Mathematics · attendance flag.
Yasmin: happy + settled · spring concert highlight.

Three things to celebrate · one to support · two ideas for May. Open the Dashboard to read each in full.
